GEX explosion across major indices with SPY net GEX surging $1.16T to $6.15T, hitting 97th percentile extremes

Market Intelligence Brief
Generated 11:32 ET
Index puts spike while single-name calls surge as dispersion trade unfolds
KEY TAKEAWAYS
  • 1. Extreme P/C divergence (index 1.96 vs single-name 0.77) signals institutional dispersion hedging into earnings week
  • 2. Low implied correlation (0.06) confirms stock-picking environment with single-name vol trading more efficiently than index
  • 3. Massive SPY put blocks ahead of monthly OpEx Friday create potential squeeze catalyst if market holds above $677
NARRATIVE — dispersion opportunity bullish

The most striking signal today is the extreme divergence between index and single-name option demand, with index P/C spiking to 1.96 while single-name P/C compressed to 0.77 — the widest spread in weeks. This pattern typically reflects institutional dispersion hedging: buying index puts as portfolio insurance while accumulating individual stock calls for earnings plays. The 387,477 SPY Apr 10 680P block and 288,476 SPY 681P block represent massive downside protection, yet single-names saw aggressive call accumulation in NVDA (203k contracts at 187.5 strike) and speculative names like DBD (+19,881% call volume). Implied correlation plunged to just 0.06, confirming a stock-specific regime where individual name volatility trades more efficiently than index vol — exactly what the P/C split suggests. This creates a clear dispersion opportunity, with vol divergence flags highlighting cheap single-name vol in XBI, KWEB, and XLK while index protection remains expensive. The setup is reinforced by positive dealer gamma of $7.3B in SPY, which should dampen broad market moves while allowing individual stocks to break out around earnings. Energy stands out with unusual cross-currents: XLE saw a massive 10,000 contract Sep 50C block at $8.35 while showing vol high/skew flat divergence, suggesting crude positioning ahead of seasonal demand. With GS and JPM earnings Monday-Tuesday, the current vol backwardation in both names suggests elevated near-term event risk is already priced, creating potential fade opportunities if results disappoint the elevated expectations.

Layer 1: Sentiment +16Layer 2: GEX +$7.3BLayer 3: P/C divergence 1.96/0.77Layer 4: IC 0.06 stock-specific

GREEK PRESSURE KEY
+$M = dealer buys to re-hedge
−$M = dealer sells to re-hedge
Charm: time-decay drift only. Vanna: vol-shift only. Not blended.
